title: Pterocyclus angelicoides
taxonomy: Pterocyclus angelicoides is a species of air-breathing land snail, a terrestrial pulmonate gastropod mollusk in the family Rhipidodontidae. It is a type of snail that belongs to the phylum Mollusca and the class Gastropoda. The classification of Pterocyclus angelicoides includes its order, which is Stylommatophora, a group of air-breathing snails. This classification also includes its family, Rhipidodontidae, and its genus, Pterocyclus. The species is further classified into its kingdom, Animalia, which includes all multicellular, eukaryotic animals.

intro: Pterocyclus angelicoides, also known as Angelica Dahurica, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Asia, characterized by its tall stem and umbrella-shaped clusters of small white flowers. Traditionally, Angelica Dahurica has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Angelica Dahurica include ferulic acid, feruloylglucoside, and polyacetylenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Angelica Dahurica is often prepared as a dried root or made into teas, tinctures, or capsules to leverage its health benefits.
